examples.title: FCFS Disk Scheduling Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of First Come First Serve disk scheduling
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FCFS_Schedule(requests, start_head)
BEGIN
  total_seek_time = 0
  current_head = start_head
  
  FOR each request IN requests DO
    seek_distance = ABS(request.track - current_head)
    total_seek_time = total_seek_time + seek_distance
    current_head = request.track
    PRINT "Serviced request at track", request.track
  END FOR
  
  PRINT "Total seek time:", total_seek_time
  PRINT "Average seek time:", total_seek_time / requests.length
END
examples.explanation: FCFS processes requests in the order they arrive. It is simple and fair but does not optimize seek time since the head may move back and forth across the disk.

examples.title: SSTF Disk Scheduling Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of Shortest Seek Time First scheduling
examples.code:
ALGORITHM SSTF_Schedule(requests, start_head)
BEGIN
  total_seek_time = 0
  current_head = start_head
  remaining_requests = requests.copy()
  
  WHILE remaining_requests IS NOT empty DO
    nearest = find_nearest(current_head, remaining_requests)
    seek_distance = ABS(nearest.track - current_head)
    total_seek_time = total_seek_time + seek_distance
    current_head = nearest.track
    REMOVE nearest FROM remaining_requests
    PRINT "Serviced request at track", nearest.track
  END WHILE
  
  PRINT "Total seek time:", total_seek_time
END

FUNCTION find_nearest(head, req_list)
BEGIN
  min_distance = INFINITY
  nearest = NULL
  FOR each req IN req_list DO
    distance = ABS(req.track - head)
    IF distance < min_distance THEN
      min_distance = distance
      nearest = req
    END IF
  END FOR
  RETURN nearest
END
examples.explanation: SSTF selects the request closest to the current head position to minimize seek distance. It performs better than FCFS but may cause starvation for requests far from the current position.

examples.title: SCAN Disk Scheduling Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of SCAN (Elevator) algorithm in Python
examples.code:
def scan_scheduler(requests, start_head, disk_size=200, direction="right"):
    """
    SCAN Disk Scheduling Algorithm
    Head moves in one direction servicing all requests,
    then reverses direction
    """
    total_seek_time = 0
    current_head = start_head
    
    # Sort requests and add boundary values
    sorted_requests = sorted(requests)
    
    # Split requests into those before and after head
    left = [r for r in sorted_requests if r < current_head]
    right = [r for r in sorted_requests if r >= current_head]
    
    # Add end of disk for complete sweep
    if direction == "right":
        service_order = right + left[::-1]
    else:
        service_order = left[::-1] + right
    
    print(f"SCAN Scheduling from head {current_head}")
    print(f"Direction: {direction}")
    print("-" * 40)
    
    for request in service_order:
        seek_distance = abs(request - current_head)
        total_seek_time += seek_distance
        current_head = request
        print(f"Serviced track {request}, seek distance: {seek_distance}")
    
    print("-" * 40)
    print(f"Total seek time: {total_seek_time}")
    print(f"Average seek time: {total_seek_time / len(requests):.2f}")
    
    return total_seek_time

# Example usage
requests = [98, 183, 37, 122, 14, 124, 65, 67]
start_head = 53
scan_scheduler(requests, start_head)
examples.explanation: SCAN algorithm works like an elevator - head moves in one direction servicing all requests until reaching the end, then reverses. It provides better performance than FCFS and SSTF while avoiding starvation.

examples.title: Contiguous File Allocation
examples.description: Implementation of contiguous file allocation in C
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates contiguous file allocation. It finds contiguous free blocks on disk and allocates them to files. The file table maintains metadata including filename, starting block, and length.

examples.title: Free Space Management with Bit Vector
examples.description: Implementation of free space management using bitmap in Python
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates free space management using a bit vector (bitmap). Each block is represented by a bit - 0 for free and 1 for allocated. It provides O(1) time for checking if a specific block is free and efficient searching for contiguous free blocks.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for scanning, O(1) for individual operations
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of blocks
